picks.reasoning: St. James' Park is one of the most intimidating home venues in the Premier League and Newcastle consistently punish sides there. Crucially, Bournemouth are in transition: Marco Rose has just agreed a deal to replace Andoni Iraola, meaning the squad faces a significant managerial change at the end of a long season. That psychological distraction, combined with Newcastle's well-documented home strength, means Ladbrokes are underpricing the hosts. We estimate a true home win probability of 55% against an implied 48.8%.

Sheffield Wednesday are in complete institutional disarray. Their preferred bidder has been told the club faces a 15-point penalty next season, they are subject to a transfer ban until January 2027, and ownership talks with Arise Capital Partners remain unresolved. The combination of financial crisis, point deductions looming, and recruitment restrictions creates a toxic morale environment. Charlton arrive as a well-organised side with nothing of the sort to worry about. Ladbrokes price Charlton at 57.1% implied — we estimate closer to 63% given how damaging Wednesday's off-pitch chaos is on performance.

picks.match: Tottenham Hotspur vs Brighton and Hove Albion
picks.pick_label: Brighton and Hove Albion to win
picks.reasoning: Tottenham's entire transfer strategy is contingent on Premier League survival — the club are reportedly signing Marcos Senesi 'subject to maintaining their Premier League status.' That is a relegation-threatened side in all but name. Brighton, by contrast, are a tactically coherent mid-table outfit with no existential pressure. They have repeatedly beaten sides under pressure this season. Ladbrokes price Brighton at 44.4% implied — we see them closer to 50% as Spurs lack the belief and defensive stability to grind out home results right now.
